title: Warmly Charred Norwegian Vegetable Skillet
description: A light and flavorful Norwegian dish featuring garlic sauce and a variety of charred vegetables, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This plant-based friendly recipe is made with pressure-cooked vegetables and a molten garlic sauce.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped red onion and cook for 5 minutes, or until it starts to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the chopped carrots and Brussels sprouts to the skillet, stirring to combine with the onion and garlic mixture.
Cook the vegetables for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they start to char and soften.
Add the vegetable broth to the skillet and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the vegetables for 10 minutes, or until they are tender and the liquid has reduced slightly.
Stir in the nutritional yeast, if using, and season the vegetables with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the vegetables hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
